chapter: Chapter: Keeping Time with the Skies

questions.question: Which natural object helped early humans measure time during the day?
questions.explanation: Early humans observed the Sun’s position in the sky to estimate time during the day.

questions.question: What causes day and night on Earth?
questions.explanation: Day and night occur due to Earth's rotation on its axis.

questions.question: How long does Earth take to complete one rotation?
questions.explanation: Earth takes approximately 24 hours to complete one rotation.

questions.question: Which device was traditionally used to tell time using shadows?
questions.explanation: A sundial tells time based on the position of the Sun and the shadow it creates.

questions.question: Which natural object changes its shape during the month?
questions.explanation: The Moon appears to change shape during the lunar cycle.

questions.question: What is the time taken by the Moon to complete one cycle of phases?
questions.explanation: The lunar cycle takes about 29.5 days.

questions.question: Which phase of the Moon occurs when the Moon is not visible from Earth?
questions.explanation: During a New Moon the Moon is not visible from Earth.

questions.question: Which phase occurs when the Moon appears fully illuminated?
questions.explanation: During a Full Moon the entire face of the Moon visible from Earth is illuminated.

questions.question: What is a constellation?

options.B: A group of stars forming a pattern

questions.explanation: Constellations are groups of stars forming recognizable patterns in the sky.

questions.question: Which constellation is also called the Great Bear?
questions.explanation: Ursa Major is commonly known as the Great Bear.

questions.question: Which constellation contains the well-known star pattern called the Big Dipper?
questions.explanation: The Big Dipper is part of the Ursa Major constellation.

questions.question: What helps astronomers measure time and seasons using the sky?
questions.explanation: Astronomers observe moon phases and star movements to measure time and seasons.

questions.question: What is a calendar mainly used for?
questions.explanation: Calendars organize time into days, months and years.

questions.question: Which movement of Earth causes the year?
questions.explanation: Earth’s revolution around the Sun causes the yearly cycle.

questions.question: How long does Earth take to complete one revolution around the Sun?
questions.explanation: Earth takes about 365 days to complete one revolution.

questions.question: Which star helps people find direction in the night sky?
questions.explanation: Polaris helps locate the north direction.

questions.question: Which natural event helped ancient people measure months?
questions.explanation: The lunar cycle helped ancient people measure months.

questions.question: Which object appears to move across the sky during the night?
questions.explanation: Stars appear to move due to Earth's rotation.

questions.question: Which branch of science studies objects in space?
questions.explanation: Astronomy studies stars, planets, and other celestial objects.

questions.question: Which natural cycle helped ancient civilizations develop calendars?
questions.explanation: Moon cycles helped create early lunar calendars.

questions.question: What causes shadows to change length during the day?
questions.explanation: As Earth rotates, the Sun’s position appears to change, altering shadow length.

questions.question: Which object in the sky is visible during the daytime?
questions.explanation: The Sun is visible during the day.

questions.question: Which object reflects sunlight to appear bright at night?
questions.explanation: The Moon reflects sunlight which makes it visible at night.

questions.question: Which natural object helped sailors navigate in ancient times?
questions.explanation: The North Star helped sailors determine direction.

questions.question: Why do we see different constellations in different seasons?
questions.explanation: Earth’s revolution changes the part of the sky visible at night during different seasons.
